comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: This comment addresses the proposed reconsideration of the 2024 EtO sterilizer standards. The proposal raises concerns regarding the adequacy of the agency’s explanation for reversing recent determinations and the sufficiency of the supporting record. First, the proposal relies on a revised statutory interpretation to justify rescission of the residual risk ...

My stepmother, Neila Campbell, was a pillar of the community, who led trauma response teams at local schools, churches, and other organizations following devestating sudden deaths including car accidents and school shootings. She helped people rebuild their lives and held the community together in the wake of tragedy. She had never smoked cigarettes, but she...

I oppose the relaxation of standards as proposed. Ethylene oxide is a known human carcinogen and exposure can cause of asthma. The proposed "reconsideration" is opposite the published scientific determinations for exposure. The "reconsideration" is purely a political stunt that does not rely on science and medicine. By increasing the risk to the health and s...

I grew up in east Texas and was exposed to ethylene oxide most of my childhood. I was 30 years old when I found my breast cancer and now 11 years later I’m dying. I’m leaving my 2 kids, spouse, family & friends behind soon. I lived a healthy lifestyle and are well. I was an accountant who loved travel and contributed to our society. Me and so many people lik...

I oppose weakening these standards regulating the pollution of our environment with known carcinogens. Your administration was elected to make America healthy again and you are actively doing the opposite. The American people and MAHA are watching. Do not break your promises to the American people.

Curious if anyone reading this has ever had cancer? I have. And the chemotherapy it takes to get rid of it is hell. I will never ever forget running my fingers through my hair after my first infusion and having clumps of my hair just fall out. Then it was my eyebrows. I was unrecognizable to myself…a shell of a human. Not to mention this emotional toll it to...
